researchInput.researchBrief: ProdiaのStep1は、毎回ゼロからGitHub/ハッカソン/プロダクトギャラリーを調べ直すのではなく、永続的な productSourceIndex と当日の currentTopicRadar を整理してStep2へ渡す。productSourceIndexは、最近のハッカソン受賞作、GitHubで最近注目され始めている小型/中型プロジェクト、Product Hunt・Hugging Face Spaces・indie galleryなどで急上昇している小型プロダクトを辞書のように蓄積する。currentTopicRadarは、技術・社会情勢・SDGs/公共・教育・研究・文化/スポーツ・生活者行動など、毎回新しく調べるトピックを保持する。

persistentProductSourceIndex.purpose: 小さくても面白い視点を持つプロダクトのコンセプト、課題、価値構造、入力、出力、UI/AIメカニズム、受けた理由、コピー禁止境界を蓄積する。
persistentProductSourceIndex.dedupeRule: canonical URLを最優先し、なければ normalized name + source category で重複判定する。同じプロダクトなら追加せず、証拠・価値ナレッジ・lastSeenAtを更新する。

dailyTopicRadar.purpose: 今掛け合わせると意味が出る技術・社会・教育・研究・文化・生活トピックを集める。これはインデックスとして長期蓄積するより、鮮度重視で毎回更新する。

step1Role.name: Research Editor / Material Curator
do: productSourceIndexから、Step2で使う価値コア候補を選ぶ
currentTopicRadarから、掛け合わせ先として使えるトピックを選ぶ
古い・弱い・重複・大手プロダクト由来の情報を降格する
sourceProductCards、valueKnowledgeCards、sourceArchiveIndex、topicCardsを後段が使いやすい形に整理する
doNot: 毎回3ソースをゼロから調査し直す
メジャープロダクトをsourceProductCardsにする
要件定義やUI仕様を書く

productSourceIndexEntryFrame.whyTheseFieldsMatter: Step2で、既存の小型プロダクトのアイデア根幹をSCAMPERやアナロジー転用でずらし、当日トピックと掛け合わせるため。

topicRadarFrame.whyTheseFieldsMatter: Step2で、プロダクト価値コアの移植先・掛け算相手として使うため。

primarySourceCategories.label: 最近のハッカソン受賞作品
primarySourceCategories.goal: 世界中の最近のハッカソン受賞作、finalist、demo showcaseから、小さいが鋭い入力→変換→出力の型を蓄積する。

primarySourceCategories.label: GitHubで最近注目され始めているもの
primarySourceCategories.goal: GitHub Trending、recently active repos、Show HN/開発者コミュニティで話題化している小型/中型OSSから、プロダクトとして転用できるコア構造を蓄積する。

primarySourceCategories.label: プロダクトギャラリー/紹介サイトで急上昇しているもの
primarySourceCategories.goal: Product Hunt、Hugging Face Spaces、indie maker launch、AI tool directory、public product galleryから、反応・伸び・デモ映えが見える小さな公開作品を蓄積する。

ideaGenerationPrincipleForStep2.base: productSourceIndexにある既存の小型プロダクト/ハッカソン作品のアイデア根幹を使う。
ideaGenerationPrincipleForStep2.move: SCAMPER、アナロジー転用、逆転、対象ユーザー変更、出力形式変更、スケール変更などで、元アイデアをずらす。
ideaGenerationPrincipleForStep2.multiply: ずらした価値コアに currentTopicRadar のトピックを掛け合わせる。
ideaGenerationPrincipleForStep2.review: IdeatorとReviewerのサブチームで、新規性、面白さ、一般ユーザーへの分かりやすさ、AIらしさ、実現性、安全性をスコアリングして修正する。

constraints: 外部APIやログインを必須にしない
長い本文をコピーしない
URLと短い根拠要約を残す
観測事実と解釈を分ける
Researcherは最終コンセプトを決めない
実在作品を丸ごとコピーしない
メジャープロダクトをsourceProductCardsにしない
同じプロダクトはインデックス上で重複登録せず更新する
トピックは鮮度重視で毎回更新し、長期の価値ナレッジとは分ける
後段が企画化できるよう、素材タグや使われそうな文脈は残す

baselineSignals.title: W杯 日本は引き分け グループ2位で決勝トーナメントへ【詳報】
baselineSignals.summary: サッカーワールドカップ北中米大会で、日本は、1次リーグの第3戦でスウェーデンと対戦し、1対1で引き分けました。この結果、日本は1次リーグを1勝2引き分けで勝ち点を「5」とし、オランダに次ぐグループFの2位となって決勝トーナメント進出を決めました。決勝トーナメントの1回戦では、世界ランキング6位でグループCの1位、ブラジルと対戦します。記事後半では、監督・選手の談話のほか、試合の詳しい経過をお伝えしています。

reports.title: AI education interest is shifting from access to motivation, teacher support, and governance

sources.title: AI Can't Fix the Student-Motivation Problem
sources.evidenceSummary: The article argues that AI tutors have not solved student engagement and that motivation remains social and relational.

sources.title: Grounding AI-in-Education Development in Teachers' Voices
sources.evidenceSummary: A survey of Indonesian K-12 teachers reports uneven AI adoption, preparation-work benefits, and constraints around generic outputs and infrastructure.

sources.title: ChatGPT vs Teachers vs Students
sources.evidenceSummary: A large Reddit discourse analysis finds education AI debates center on integrity, enforcement, career anxiety, and cross-role conflict.

observedFacts: Education AI discourse now includes motivation, teacher workload, inequality, integrity enforcement, and student-teacher conflict.
Teacher-centered research suggests AI is used for preparation and materials, but contextual fit remains a problem.
Reddit discourse research suggests adversarial integrity themes drive more engagement than constructive integration.
reports.interpretation: AI education is not simply a tutoring or content-generation story. The live tension is around motivation, trust, teacher burden, and whether AI creates or reduces social friction.
reports.trendSignal: The education lane is moving from 'AI can teach' toward 'how do humans, institutions, and AI negotiate learning behavior?'
reports.audienceReaction: Teachers, students, parents, and administrators appear divided between efficiency gains, cheating fears, and concern that AI tools miss relational learning needs.
reports.underlyingMechanism: Learning depends on motivation, context, social accountability, and institutional rules; generic AI assistance does not automatically solve those mechanisms.
uncertainties: The Atlantic article is commentary and should be paired with empirical sources.
Reddit discourse may overrepresent adversarial or highly engaged communities.
Indonesia teacher survey findings may not transfer directly to Japan or the US.
riskNotes: Avoid making educational efficacy claims.
Avoid replacing teacher judgment or giving school policy advice.
Use education signals as discussion material, not as validated pedagogy.

reports.title: Browser agents and AI extensions create a trust surface, not only an automation surface

sources.title: Malicious GenAI Chrome Extensions
sources.evidenceSummary: A study of AI-themed Chrome extensions reports malicious behaviors such as data exfiltration, impersonation, redirection, and bait-and-switch updates.

sources.title: Project Mariner
sources.evidenceSummary: Google DeepMind's browser-agent research prototype explored AI agents acting across browser tasks with user oversight.

observedFacts: Baseline Product Hunt signals included BrowserAct and compact browser/action agent positioning.
AI-themed browser extensions are a known risk surface in recent research.
Browser-agent research emphasizes web navigation and user intervention, but market launches may compress that nuance into simple automation promises.
reports.interpretation: The browser is becoming a major agent surface, and browser-agent usefulness must be researched together with permission, impersonation, and data-exposure risk.
reports.trendSignal: AI agent adoption is entering the browser, where small tools can act directly on user context and therefore need trust literacy.
reports.audienceReaction: Users want friction reduction; security researchers and operators worry about extension permissions, data access, and malicious lookalikes.
reports.underlyingMechanism: Browser extensions and browser agents sit close to user sessions, pages, cookies, forms, and personal workflows, so small UX decisions can change safety boundaries.
uncertainties: The specific Product Hunt products were not audited.
Project Mariner information is research/prototype context and not directly comparable to third-party extensions.
Chrome Web Store live ranking was not independently collected.
riskNotes: Avoid accusing specific products of malicious behavior.
Do not provide security guarantees.
Frame as literacy and risk classification, not malware detection.

reports.title: World Cup attention is becoming participatory through collectibles, fairness debates, and casual-fan navigation

sources.title: World Cup fans wait hours for must-have free collectible
sources.evidenceSummary: Fans are reportedly waiting hours for customizable World Cup collectible bracelets, with city-specific charms and secondary-market behavior.

sources.title: The five biggest storylines from the 2026 FIFA World Cup so far
sources.evidenceSummary: The article frames the tournament around attendance, fan festivals, underdog teams, stars, and criticism of commercialized hydration breaks.

sources.title: A Four-Section Bracket for the 48-team World Cup
sources.evidenceSummary: A research paper analyzes bracket complexity and fairness issues in the expanded 48-team World Cup.

observedFacts: Baseline Japan trend already captured Japan's tournament progression.
External sources show collectible behavior, fan festival scale, tournament expansion, and fairness/format complexity.
The World Cup creates both emotional participation and structural confusion for casual fans.
reports.interpretation: World Cup research material is stronger when treated as a participation system: fans collect, predict, argue about fairness, navigate brackets, and attach identity to places and teams.
reports.trendSignal: Large sports events can produce playful and educational artifacts because they combine rules, scarcity, identity, and public conversation.
reports.audienceReaction: Fans show excitement, queueing behavior, collectible desire, frustration with commercial decisions, and curiosity about tournament paths.
reports.underlyingMechanism: Scarcity, bracket complexity, national identity, and city-specific experiences turn passive watching into a multi-layer participation game.
conceptSeeds: collectible scarcity and city identity map
uncertainties: Some fan-culture sources are media reports and may exaggerate novelty.
Tournament data changes daily and needs freshness handling.
Collectible resale claims need careful sourcing.
riskNotes: Avoid trademark-heavy product framing.
Avoid pretending to be official FIFA information.
Sports updates can become stale quickly.

reports.title: Japan weather alerts point to civic action-literacy as a recurring research lane

sources.title: NHK typhoon and warning-level articles from baseline collector
sources.evidenceSummary: Baseline Japan trend signals included typhoon approach, level-4 warnings, flooding impact, and emergency safety actions.

sources.title: NHK emergency safety action item from baseline collector
sources.evidenceSummary: Baseline signal referenced emergency safety action language in Kyoto.

observedFacts: Baseline Japan signals were dominated by weather, flooding, warning levels, and emergency action language.
The topic is timely, location-specific, and action-oriented.
The same lane also contained sports and medical service signals, showing Japan trend collection can mix urgent and non-urgent civic material.
reports.interpretation: Disaster and weather signals should be preserved as civic research material, but they require stricter safety boundaries than sports or maker trends.
reports.trendSignal: During weather events, public information needs are immediate and practical: what the warning means, what not to do, and where official guidance lives.
reports.audienceReaction: Residents, commuters, families, schools, and local operators likely need clear action interpretation under uncertainty.
reports.underlyingMechanism: Warning systems encode severity and recommended action, but ordinary users may not understand the hierarchy or local implications.
uncertainties: No official municipal pages or JMA pages were added in this exploration.
This material should be paired with official disaster agencies before any public-facing artifact.
The signal is time-sensitive and may become stale within hours.
riskNotes: Do not replace official disaster guidance.
Do not generate location-specific safety advice without official source grounding.
Avoid playful framing for active emergencies.

coverageGaps: No direct Reddit/X/Discord/TikTok/YouTube reaction data was used.
Chrome Web Store and Figma Community live ranking pages were not directly collected.
Japan civic/weather exploration did not add JMA or municipal official sources.
Exploration used a small number of web checks; it should be treated as a sample, not exhaustive Deep Research.
explorationReports.editorNotes: The best diversification value comes from education motivation, World Cup fan participation, and browser-agent trust. The education lane adds social depth; the World Cup lane adds playful/culture material; the browser-extension lane connects Product Hunt/browser-agent market patterns to safety research. Disaster-readiness material is useful but should be handled conservatively.

entries.name: Agent-Reach
entries.concept: A CLI that lets AI agents read and search multiple public web platforms without separate paid APIs.
entries.oneLineDescription: Gives an agent a common reading layer across social and developer sites.
entries.problemSolved: Agent builders often need cross-platform public context but each platform has different APIs, login boundaries, and data formats.
entries.targetUser: AI agent developers and automation builders
entries.userFriction: It treats web reach as an agent capability primitive rather than a bespoke scraper per task.
entries.coreUserInput: Search terms, target platforms, and agent research tasks.
entries.coreOutput: Normalized public content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and similar sources.
entries.outputArtifact: cross-platform research results for an agent
entries.coreMechanism: The tool wraps platform-specific scrapers and transcripts behind one CLI surface so agents can request evidence through a shared interface.
entries.interactionPattern: Developer invokes a command or tool endpoint and receives normalized snippets that can be fed into an agent workflow.
entries.whyItIsInteresting: It treats web reach as an agent capability primitive rather than a bespoke scraper per task.
entries.whyItGotAttention: GitHub API showed more than 42k stars, 2026 creation, and recent pushes, suggesting strong demand for agent browsing infrastructure.
entries.noveltyKernel: The value is not one scraper, but the common evidence interface for agents.
entries.transferableStructure: Wrap fragmented external sources behind one task-specific evidence interface with normalized outputs.
entries.ideaKernel: Many sources to one agent-readable evidence pipe
entries.cloneRisk: Copying platform scraping behavior, unsupported access methods, or the exact agent web-reach positioning.
entries.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy platform scraping implementation or any access-bypass claim; transfer only the unified evidence-interface pattern.
entries.reasonIncluded: Strong transferable structure for unifying fragmented public information sources.
entries.reasonNotMajorProduct: It is an OSS utility repo and not a dominant platform product.

discoverySources.storedEvidenceSummary: Gives an agent a common reading layer across social and developer sites.

coverageStrategy.coverageNotes: This sample exercises broad topic-card structure for Step2. Replace it with a real topic-research response before production runs.

topicCards.title: AIエージェント出力の根拠確認
topicCards.summary: AIエージェントや自動生成ワークフローの利用が広がるほど、出力がどの入力・判断・制約から来たのかを確認したい需要が強くなる。
topicCards.whyHotNow: AIを使った下書き・調査・コード生成が日常化し、作る速さよりも確認・説明・引き継ぎの摩擦が目立ちやすくなっている。

targetAudience.segment: AIを業務や制作に使う個人・小チーム
targetAudience.userMoment: AIが作った成果物をレビュー、共有、再利用する前

topicCards.userFrictionOrDesire: 結果は得られるが、どこを信じてよいか、何を再確認すべきか、次の人にどう渡すかが分かりにくい。
topicCards.friction: 入力、判断根拠、未確認事項、リスクを短時間で切り分けられない。
topicCards.remixUse: Step2 can pair this with source cores that expose evidence, score confidence, or turn opaque outputs into reviewable artifacts.
topicCards.riskBoundary: Do not claim to prove truth, security, authorship, or compliance. Keep uncertainty, missing evidence, and human review visible.
riskNotes: Avoid security or legal guarantees.
Do not hide missing evidence behind a single confidence score.

topicCards.title: 家庭と地域の災害・猛暑準備
topicCards.summary: 災害、猛暑、停電、避難などについて、一般情報を家庭や地域ごとの具体行動に落としたい需要がある。
topicCards.whyHotNow: 季節性と公共性があり、天候・地域通知・家庭事情によって必要な準備が変わるため、生活者が自分ごと化しやすい。

targetAudience.segment: 子ども、高齢者、ペット、移動制約のある家族を持つ生活者
targetAudience.userMoment: 注意喚起や自治体通知を見たが、家庭で何を確認すべきか整理したい時

topicCards.userFrictionOrDesire: 防災情報は多いが、自分の住居、家族構成、持ち物、地域事情に合わせた準備リストに変換しにくい。
topicCards.friction: 公式情報を読んでも、今日できる確認事項、買い足す物、家族内で決めることに分解しにくい。
topicCards.remixUse: Source product cores that turn messy information into action cards, ranked plans, or printable packs can transfer well.
topicCards.riskBoundary: Do not present generated output as official emergency guidance or replace local authority instructions.
riskNotes: Show missing information and source uncertainty.
Avoid playful framing for active emergencies.

topicCards.title: 学校・保護者のお知らせ理解
topicCards.summary: 学校、塾、自治体からの長いお知らせを、家庭ごとの行動や確認事項に変える需要がある。
topicCards.whyHotNow: 教育、家庭、地域の接点にあり、非エンジニアにも価値が伝わりやすい。年度行事、休校、提出物、支払いなどで繰り返し発生する。

targetAudience.segment: 保護者・学生・学校事務担当
targetAudience.userMoment: 複数のお知らせ、締切、持ち物、提出先を整理したい時

topicCards.userFrictionOrDesire: 文章は読めても、いつ何をすればいいか、誰に確認すればいいかが分かりにくい。
topicCards.friction: 予定、持ち物、支払い、同意、連絡先が文章内に散らばり、家庭内共有もしづらい。
topicCards.remixUse: Expert-to-novice explanation, paperwork workflow, and action-card patterns can transfer well.
topicCards.riskBoundary: Do not alter official instructions, invent deadlines, or act as the school authority.
riskNotes: Keep source snippets and uncertainty visible.
Make translation or simplification clearly secondary to the original notice.

topicCards.title: 個人クリエイターの公開前チェック
topicCards.summary: 動画、投稿、LP、ニュースレター、イベント告知などを公開する前に、伝わるか、弱点はないかを見たい需要がある。
topicCards.whyHotNow: 個人や小チームの発信量が増え、AIで作った素材をそのまま出す前の判断や編集が課題になっている。

targetAudience.segment: 個人クリエイター、同人・イベント運営、小規模マーケター
targetAudience.userMoment: 公開直前に、伝わり方、誤解、弱い訴求を確認したい時

topicCards.userFrictionOrDesire: 作ることは簡単になったが、出す価値があるか、誰に刺さるか、どこが弱いかは判断しにくい。
topicCards.friction: 自分の意図と初見の受け取り方のズレを、公開前に短時間で見つけにくい。
topicCards.remixUse: Constraint scoring, multi-role creative output, and after-event explanation patterns can become preflight evaluators.
topicCards.riskBoundary: Do not promise virality, platform performance, or legal clearance.
riskNotes: Make critique concrete rather than generic.
Avoid imitating a specific creator's brand without permission.

topicCards.title: 自治体・公共サービス手続きの入口整理
topicCards.summary: 補助金、証明書、子育て、介護、引っ越しなどの公共手続きについて、自分が何を確認し、どこから始めるべきか知りたい需要がある。
topicCards.whyHotNow: 生活イベントごとに必要手続きが変わり、自治体サイトやPDFが複数に分かれているため、日常の小さな混乱が発生し続けている。

targetAudience.segment: 引っ越し、育児、介護、申請を抱える生活者
targetAudience.userMoment: 公式ページを見たが、自分に関係する手続きと順番を整理したい時

topicCards.userFrictionOrDesire: 制度名や条件が分かりにくく、自分が対象か、どの書類が必要か、何から読むべきか判断しにくい。
topicCards.friction: 公式情報の入口、対象条件、必要書類、次の確認先がばらばらで、行動に移る前に疲れる。
topicCards.remixUse: Step2 can combine this with source cores that simplify official information, rank next actions, or preserve source-grounded explanations.
topicCards.riskBoundary: Do not replace official advice, determine eligibility as fact, submit forms, or impersonate a government service.
riskNotes: Cite official sources when available.
Flag when a user must confirm with the responsible office.

currentTopicRadar.editorNotesForRemixStrategist: Each topic card keeps legacy keys for Step2 and adds targetAudience, friction, riskBoundary, and bestFitSourceMechanisms so source value cores can be matched without turning topics into product concepts.
